intent: test an Android app end-to-end against specs on a local Docker emulator
finding: The user drove a long manual loop to test the Android app inside budtmo/docker-android: enable KVM, rebuild the APK, install via adb, skip onboarding, tap through gates using hard-coded screen coordinates, and dump uiautomator XML to verify each state. Each iteration required several tool calls the assistant had to re-derive from scratch. The work is reproducible but not encoded anywhere as a script or skill, so the same dance will recur for every UI change.
existing_mitigation: infra/emulator docker-compose exists; KVM was confirmed working this session — but onboarding/install/tap/verify flow has no scripted or skill-level wrapper.

intent: reconnect a physical Android phone over wireless ADB when the debug port rotates
finding: Wireless ADB port changes every time the phone's Wireless Debugging toggle is flipped, so the user had to read the port off the phone screen and paste it back into the session 7+ times in one sitting — with visible frustration. Tailscale is in place for the link but the port discovery step is still fully manual. A small auto-reconnect helper (scan Tailscale IP + last-known port, fall back to mDNS/QR or prompt-for-port) would eliminate a recurring micro-interrupt the user experiences every time they test on-device.
existing_mitigation: Tailscale already carries the adb traffic; the `connect-phone` helper script exists but still requires the port as an argument.

intent: refresh a freshly renamed Expo/React-Native app on a phone without stale-cache artifacts
finding: After renaming the Android namespace/applicationId, `./gradlew clean` did NOT remove `android/build/generated/autolinking/autolinking.json`, and the debug/release keystore mismatch forced a manual uninstall before reinstall. The user discovered both gotchas interactively. Either a rename-cleanup helper or a documented 'first install after rename' checklist would prevent the next occurrence.
existing_mitigation: None — the session ended with the manual workaround but no script, doc, or skill note was added.

intent: run the Mint-to-NixOS drift cron reliably so config drift is surfaced without manual intervention
finding: The mint-drift cron failed silently for days: cron's restricted PATH couldn't find `claude`/`nix`, a bash variable in the prompt text was being expanded and corrupted the JSON payload, and a python3 hook kept looping. The user had to run the script manually 3+ times while iterating the fixes. The automation exists but its reliability surface (PATH, quoting, hook interactions) is underspecified and untested.
existing_mitigation: `nixos-config/scripts/mint-drift-agent.sh` exists and is now patched, but there is no smoke-test or cron-dry-run harness to catch regressions in the PATH/quoting layer.

intent: reclaim disk space when the SSD is near-full
finding: Disk-full triggered a manual `du | sort` hunt across /home, /var, and docker volumes. The session ended up purging docker images/volumes, gradle caches, and huggingface model caches by hand. This is the second time this pattern shows up in recent logs and is entirely scriptable (per-cache purge commands + a dry-run-first safety flag).
existing_mitigation: None — a one-shot `/disk-reclaim` skill or script would replace the guided tour with a single invocation.

intent: restore Claude Code marketplace list after known_marketplaces.json is reset or corrupted
finding: `known_marketplaces.json` got clobbered and the user re-added 8 marketplaces by hand (`claude plugins marketplace add ...` × 8). A tiny idempotent restore script seeded from a checked-in manifest would make this a no-op.
existing_mitigation: `extraKnownMarketplaces` in settings covers part of this but the runtime state file still diverges and requires manual re-seeding.

intent: install the Everything-Claude-Code suite into a project-local isolated config
finding: Setting up ECC in a project-scoped config required chaining HOME-redirected install, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, a bespoke .envrc, gitignore additions, and manual settings-hook cleanup. The user remarked they 'zapped it this morning' — implying this is not the first time. The sequence is deterministic enough to encode as a bootstrap script or skill with a single argument (the target repo path).
existing_mitigation: ECC's own `install-apply.js` exists; the orchestration around it (direnv + CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR + .envrc + gitignore) is what's missing.

intent: lock down destructive ADB operations so prompt-injection can't wipe a device
finding: The user layered a dcg.toml destructive-command guard, settings deny-list, and hook-based lockdown on the agent's own config files. The safeguards were assembled piecewise during the session. A small hardening checklist (or template) for 'projects that talk to a real device' would make future device-touching projects secure-by-default rather than secure-by-remembering.
existing_mitigation: dcg plugin + per-project settings deny list now in place on jhana; not generalized to other on-device projects.

intent: debug a router-agent worker whose MCP proxy fails to expose tools to a headless Claude
finding: The user explicitly asked for a 'move one item, see if it works, repeat' loop to isolate why child MCPs weren't surfacing tools in the worker: KeyError in `_discover_proxied_tools` when any child MCP failed, HOME=SCRATCH propagating into MCP children and breaking ~ resolution, and deferred MCP tools needing explicit `ToolSearch` in allowedTools. None of these had regression tests; the diagnosis depended on the user walking Claude through each layer.
existing_mitigation: locked-down-headless-agent skill was updated with the learnings at session end; no automated smoke test yet verifies a worker can list its proxied tools on startup.

intent: kill every running Android emulator on the machine (docker + native) in one command
finding: Mid-debug the user needed all emulators dead to free RAM and had to hunt docker containers and native qemu processes separately. Small, self-contained helper — worth capturing as a one-liner script or alias.
